notes: Lesson 22 Part 1 (act 129), 'The machine via Next Greater Element', 75.19s, 6 cues. arr=[8,5,3,7,9], nge=[9,7,7,9,-1]. New reusable monotonic-stack idiom (arrays.py: stack_base/stack_cell/stack_pos/recolor_stack_cell): a vertical violet index-stack (values decreasing top->bottom), blue newcomer, emerald pop=resolve, an emerald arrow recording the answer. Two iterations: (1) authored + manim -s smoke all 6 cues READ (caught cue03 amort-note overlapping the stack label -> lifted STACK_BASE and narrowed/left-aligned the note), (2) full -ql render + READ start/mid/end of every cue from the muxed mp4. Per-cue checklist (stack decreasing-invariant correctness, push-just-waits vs pop-resolves pedagogy, cascade resolves several, leftovers->-1, O(n) bound, safe-area guard, chunk timing all Δ<=0.066s pinned): all pass. Minor: cue03 mid-frame has the transient floating-newcomer label near the array top (fully legible, end-state clean).

frames.notes: array fading in at t=0.15s (start of FadeIn)
arr [8,5,3,7,9] + nge '?' row + stack base, pushing i0=8
i0=8 on stack (violet), 'values decreasing top->bottom' caption
'7 > 3' — top popped emerald, emerald arrow records nge[2]=7
'7 > 5 pop again nge[1]=7' cascade; nge[1]=nge[2]=7, stack shrinking to [8]; transient newcomer label near array (legible)
'7 < 8 order restored — push 7'; stack [8,7], amort note clear of stack label
'index 4 is the tallest — nothing beats it'
'every index enters once, leaves at most once'
O(n^2) pair scan -> O(n) MathTex, emerald O(n), legible
